role_title: Senior Technical Product Manager - Conversational AI

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in product management
Track record of successfully launching and managing SaaS products or platforms
Comp range $130K-$150K base, consistent with Senior IC at a public enterprise SaaS company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or a related field (MBA preferred).
7+ years of experience in product management, with a track record of successfully launching and managing SaaS products or platforms.
track record of successfully launching and managing SaaS products or platforms.
Deep understanding of Conversational AI systems, including natural language processing (NLP), Large Language Models (LLMs), dialog management, and the underlying technical architecture.
Proven ability to develop and execute product strategy, prioritize features, and drive product roadmap planning in a fast-paced, agile environment.
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to gather and interpret data, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions
Excellent communication, collaboration, and leadership skills, with the ability to influence and inspire cross-functional teams.
Experience working with enterprise customers and a strong customer focus, with the ability to empathize with customer needs and pain points.
Passion for innovation, creativity, and solving complex problems to deliver impactful solutions in the rapidly evolving Conversational AI industry.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Having experience in AI/ML-centric SaaS products or platforms in the enterprise space is plus
Having AI performance metrics (e.g., resolution rate, accuracy, latency) is plus.

scope.surface: Conversational AI platform including Conversational Builder, Agent Assist, KAI, and related AI infrastructure
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B SaaS, conversational AI platform
core_responsibilities: Develop and articulate a clear product vision and strategy for Conversational AI Teams
Define, review feature requests, and prioritize product features and enhancements
Create and maintain a detailed product roadmap with key milestones, dependencies, and timelines
Work closely with Engineering, Infrastructure, Cloud, AI/ML teams, Sales, and Customer Success
Champion a platform-first approach for high availability, low latency, and scalability
Conduct ongoing research and analysis on emerging trends in Conversational AI and LLMs
Engage with enterprise customers to gather insights and translate feedback into product improvements
Partner with Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success on go-to-market strategies
Define KPIs and metrics to measure effectiveness of Conversational AI platform capabilities
Stay informed about advancements in Conversational AI systems, LLMs, and high-scale AI infrastructure

compensation.equity_form: Employee Stock Purchase Program (ESPP) with discount on company shares

green_flags: Fully remote role with clear LI-Remote tag
Company is publicly traded (NASDAQ: LPSN) with established enterprise product
Named as #1 Most Innovative AI Company by Fast Company — strong employer brand signal
Comprehensive benefits package including 401(k) match, ESPP, generous parental leave
Gartner recognized as a leader in Conversational AI space
Clear product surface and responsibilities outlined
red_flags: Location pay differential mentioned, which may reduce comp for non-high-cost-of-living areas
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for non-US candidates despite 'USA Remote' listing
JD lists 'MBA preferred' alongside bachelor's as a soft requirement but places it in the hard-requirements section — may signal preference for overqualified candidates
Seven-plus years minimum is a high bar that may indicate a stretch role or position that has been open a while
Phishing warning included suggests active targeting of LivePerson applicants — candidates should exercise caution
